evals.prompt: Open a PR for this branch. I've implemented the core/errors submodule — typed error hierarchy, M3LResult, and tests. Everything passes: lint, typecheck, test, build.
evals.expected_output: PR created with a feat: title naming the submodule, a Summary listing the actual exported symbols, a Test plan checklist reflecting the real files changed, and a Notes line stating 'feat: → minor bump'.
expectations: Runs 'pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm build' before pushing
Pushes the branch with 'git push -u origin HEAD'
PR title uses Conventional Commit format and is ≤70 characters
PR body Summary bullets name actual symbols or files (not vague paraphrases)
PR body includes a Test plan section with a markdown checklist
PR body Notes line states the commit type and the semver impact

evals.prompt: Make a PR — I renamed M3LPaths.outputDir to M3LPaths.archiveDir. This is a breaking change; consumers need to update after upgrading.
evals.expected_output: PR created with a feat!: title, Summary explaining the rename, a Test plan that includes a typecheck step to catch old usage, and Notes stating 'feat!: → major bump' with migration instructions.
expectations: PR title starts with 'feat!:' to signal the breaking change
PR body Summary names both the old and new property (outputDir → archiveDir)
PR body Notes includes migration instructions for consumers
PR body Notes states 'major bump' or equivalent

evals.prompt: Ship this for review — I updated the claude-pr-review.yml workflow to skip Dependabot PRs. Only CI files changed, no library code.
evals.expected_output: PR with a ci: title, a compact Summary that names the changed workflow file, a Test plan referencing CI behaviour, and Notes stating 'ci: → no release'.
expectations: PR title starts with 'ci:' in lowercase
PR body Summary references the specific workflow file (.github/workflows/...)
PR body Notes states no release is triggered (ci: commits don't release)
Quality gates run before the push

evals.prompt: Get this merged — I'm on the main branch right now.
evals.expected_output: Skill stops with a clear error message telling the user they are on main and must branch off first before creating a PR.
expectations: Skill detects the current branch is 'main' and stops
Error message tells the user to create a feature or fix branch first
No push or PR is created
